_comment: Health-review backlog — the parking lot for MINOR SYSTEM / PIPELINE / DOC issues a session noticed but did not fix (a stale comment, a small wiring gap, a doc that needs a touch, a flaky test). Per docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md the session-end routine appends here instead of letting small issues rot. The autonomous /health-review skill drains this list every run: it triages each open item, fixes what it can, and marks it resolved. Do NOT use this for live-trading incidents or anything Tier-2/3 that needs immediate attention — raise those with the operator in chat. Three-way split (2026-05-26): STRATEGY / TRADING follow-ups go in docs/claude/performance-review-backlog.json; AI/ML EXPERIMENT follow-ups go in docs/claude/ml-review-backlog.json. Item ids here: BL-YYYYMMDD-NNN.

items.title: Stale workplan/milestone-state references in docs/claude process notes
items.description: After Phase 6 froze milestone-state.md + workplan.md as historical, a few lower-precedence docs still mention them as live: colab-workflows.md:57 ('a workplan [requirement]'), and assorted historical bug-log.md entries (BUG-059/063/064) reference them. decomposition-rules.md + auto-task-daily-trade-audit.md were given reconciliation notes; this is the residual minor tail. Bug-log entries are historical record and should stay; colab-workflows.md could be lightly reworded.
items.resolution_criteria: colab-workflows.md no longer frames the workplan as a live authority; confirm no docs/claude/* non-historical page treats milestone-state.md/workplan.md as current.
items.resolution: colab-workflows.md:57 reworded ('is a workplan violation' -> 'violates this delivery rule') so it no longer frames the frozen workplan.md as a live authority. decomposition-rules.md + auto-task-daily-trade-audit.md already carry reconciliation notes; remaining workplan/milestone-state mentions are in historical records (bug-log, milestone-state itself, INDEX) which are preserved as-is.

items.source: doc-freshness after #1933 (trader-bot menu rebuild + command scrub)
items.title: Sweep docs/claude operational pages + config comments that still reference the ~40 removed Telegram commands
items.description: PR #1933 rebuilt @bict_trading_bot to a 4-item menu and removed ~40 slash commands (/halt /resume /status /accounts[_status] /set_all_live /set_keys /risk_check /smoke_test /strategies /reload_strats /balance /trades /last5 /packages /signals /alerts /log /price /hourly /backtest* /webapp /health /vmstats /ping_test /vm /vm_write /checkpoint /sprintlet_* /new_session /test /toggle /closeall) plus the VM-runner subsystem. The 3 canonical docs (CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE-CANONICAL.md, CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md) were fixed in the same doc-freshness pass. STILL STALE (lower-precedence, current operational guides that reference removed commands as if live): docs/claude/trading-mode-flags.md (/halt /resume /set_all_live /accounts dry|live as the kill switch), docs/claude/deployment-ops.md (verification steps using /halt->/resume, /set_keys, /accounts_status, /vm, /vm_write), docs/claude/vm-operator-mode.md (the /vm + /vm_write VM-runner trust contract — that whole command surface is gone; the diag-relay + system-action access paths remain), docs/claude/comms-architecture.md (/new_session + '/halt /status' examples), docs/claude/recurring-sessions.md ('Need to halt everything? /halt'), docs/claude/prop-account-state.md (/accounts_status as the prop-check surface — now the menu Accounts snapshot / dashboard). ALSO config-file comments (NOT editable by doc-freshness — Tier-3 config files): config/strategies.yaml references '/reload_strats' and config/accounts.yaml references 'the Telegram /accounts command flips it at runtime via set_account_dry_run()' — both now describe the removed surface; the menu kill switch persists via set_account_mode.sh. Historical records (bug-log.md, audit-log.md, milestone-state.md, architecture-audit-*, checkpoints/) intentionally LEFT AS-IS (preserved as record). Map each kill/mode/halt mention to its menu equivalent; point readers at docs/TELEGRAM-SPEC.md.
items.resolution_criteria: The listed docs/claude/* operational pages no longer present removed slash commands as the current operator surface (they reference the menu + docs/TELEGRAM-SPEC.md instead); the strategies.yaml + accounts.yaml comments are corrected to describe the menu kill switch / set_account_mode.sh wire (config edits require the operator / a Tier-3-aware PR).
items.resolution: /health-review 2026-05-26: added a top-of-file deprecation banner to all 6 listed docs/claude/* operational pages (trading-mode-flags.md, deployment-ops.md, vm-operator-mode.md, comms-architecture.md, recurring-sessions.md, prop-account-state.md) — each banner explicitly notes PR #1933 changed the surface, names the removed commands referenced in the body, and points to docs/TELEGRAM-SPEC.md + the current alternatives (menu / set-account-mode / diag relay / system-actions / trainer-vm-diag). A reader can no longer be misled into treating body-text slash commands as the live operator surface. Also reworded the most prominent claims (recurring-sessions.md 'Need to halt everything? /halt' line) to the menu equivalent in-place. REMAINING (still open, requires operator / Tier-3 PR): config/strategies.yaml + config/accounts.yaml comments that describe the removed /reload_strats and /accounts surfaces — config files are not editable from /health-review.

items.source: live verification during the trader-bot rebuild session (2026-05-25)
items.title: ib_paper account has no ib_port — MES order client cannot reach IB Gateway
items.description: The live trader logs `ib_client_for(ib_paper): no ib_port set (config or IB_PORT env) — cannot reach IB Gateway.` on every tick that routes to ib_paper. IB *market data* works (the gateway is up after the 2026-05-25 restart and MES candle fetches succeed via IBMarketData on 127.0.0.1:4002), so MES ticks complete fast and the heartbeat is stable. But the ib_paper account's ORDER client (src/units/accounts/clients.py::ib_client_for) has no ib_port configured, so it cannot place orders on MES — the account effectively collects signals/data but can't execute. If MES is intended to trade (paper), config/accounts.yaml::ib_paper needs `ib_port: 4002` (or the IB_PORT env on the trader unit). Pre-existing (predates this session); surfaced during post-deploy verification. Operator was informed in chat 2026-05-25 and chose to log it rather than fix immediately.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; surface to the operator (do NOT auto-edit accounts.yaml).
items.resolution_criteria: Operator decides whether MES should place live (paper) orders. If yes, set ib_port (4002) on the ib_paper account in config/accounts.yaml (Tier-3 change, operator-approved) and confirm the trader log no longer emits 'no ib_port set' + a smoke order reaches IB Gateway. If MES is data-only by design, document that and close.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: still open. Status snapshot shows ib_paper live=true on sha 6a7aef4; no journalctl pull this review (relay queue wedged after 13 min — 3 of 6 issues never returned). The order-client gap (separate from IB market-data which works) would still emit the per-tick warning. Remains operator-gated (Tier-3); no change.
items.resolution: PR #2167 (squashed as 288d9bf) forwarded ib_host/ib_port/ib_account/ib_client_id through _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile in src/runtime/order_monitor.py. The original diagnosis attributed the warning to the ORDER client (coordinator.py::multi_account_execute calling ib_client_for(account_cfg)), but that path's account_cfg already carries ib_port via getattr(account, 'ib_port', None) at coordinator.py:1045 — confirmed by reading the code. The actual emitter was the MONITOR RECONCILER path: _load_account_cfgs_for_reconcile() built an account_cfg dict that only forwarded a subset of fields and dropped the IB connection keys, so account_open_positions(cfg) → ib_read_client_for(account) → ib_client_for(...) → account.get('ib_port') = None → warning + return None. Fired from 4 call sites in order_monitor.py (1523, 1877, 2491, 3463), each on a 60s monitor tick — hence the per-tick spam. Post-deploy verification on diag-request #2169 confirmed: 'ib_client_for warning count: 0' across 300 lines / ~5 ticks; reconciler successfully opened the IB read socket (clientId 9731 visible at 17:57:38). MES order dispatch was never actually blocked — the data-collection-only framing in the original description was incorrect.

items.title: intent_multiplexer swallows a raising strategy builder with only logger.warning — no audit/diag event, so a broken strategy is invisible in the diag surface
items.description: src/runtime/intent_multiplexer.py::_collect_intents (the live path; pipeline uses multiplexed_intents) catches a builder exception with only `logger.warning('strategy X raised ... skipping this tick')` and continues. Unlike the LEGACY multiplexer (src/runtime/pipeline.py::multiplexed_signal_builder, ~L243-252) which emits a `report('strategy_builder','exception', strategy=...)` audit event, the intent layer emits NOTHING to signal_audit.jsonl / the diag relays. Consequence surfaced this review: fade_breakout_4h has been raising 'No numeric types to aggregate' every tick (origin: the ATR/ADX pd.concat([...],axis=1).max(axis=1) in src/units/strategies/fade_breakout_4h.py around L104/118) and producing ZERO eval events / zero shadow data — but because the catch is silent, there was no `*_eval`, no exception event, and no diag trace; it was only visible in journalctl. A failing strategy should be observable from the read-only diag surface, not just the systemd journal.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or when a strategy is mysteriously silent in the audit tail.
items.resolution_criteria: intent_multiplexer._collect_intents emits a report()/audit event (e.g. 'strategy_builder'/'exception' with strategy + reason) on a builder exception, mirroring the legacy multiplexer, so a raising strategy shows up in signal_audit.jsonl and /api/diag/audit. (Code change — Tier-1 observability; not editable from /health-review which only writes docs + this backlog.) NOTE: this is the OBSERVABILITY gap only; the fade_breakout_4h crash itself is a separate Tier-3 strategy bug surfaced to the operator in the 2026-05-25 health-review response.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: code path unchanged in src/runtime/intent_multiplexer.py — observability gap still present (the silent swallow of strategy-builder exceptions). No audit pull this review (relay queue wedged) so cannot re-verify per-strategy *_eval emissions, but no journalctl evidence of a new silent strategy. Remains operator-gated (Tier-1 code change).

items.source: 2026-05-25 per-model status review (operator asked to fix the two degenerate shadow models)
items.title: MES regime models (mes-regime-5m/15m) collapse to one class (f1_volatile=0) — model/feature limitation, NOT data-blocked
items.description: mes-regime-5m-baseline-v1 + mes-regime-15m-baseline-v1 never predict 'volatile' (recall_volatile=precision_volatile=f1_volatile=0; acc ~0.49-0.53). IMPORTANTLY this is NOT the missing-MES-trade-data blocker: these train on MES market_features (price history), which IS present and BALANCED — 5m: 60957 range / 25916 volatile (~70/30); 15m: 17694/11278 (~61/39). So it will NOT self-resolve when IBKR is approved + MES starts trading. Root cause: RegimeClassifierTrainer is a per-bucket MODAL-class classifier (ml/trainers/regime_classifier.py) keyed on the backward-looking vol_bucket; on MES that backward vol_bucket does not separate the (forward-vol-based) regime_label, so every bucket is range-majority and argmax always picks 'range'. BTC works because BTC vol is autocorrelated; MES intraday isn't. Operator decision 2026-05-25: LEAVE manifests as-is for now (do NOT demote to research_only); revisit in a dedicated model-training session.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit when a model-training session is scheduled, or once richer/live MES history is available.
items.resolution_criteria: mes-regime-{5m,15m} eval shows f1_volatile > 0 with a non-trivial macro_f1, via a feature/model change (e.g. more vol buckets, probability-threshold eval instead of argmax, a stronger/continuous vol feature, or a forward-vol-aware feature). Trainer-side (autonomous) work; verify on the trainer VM before/after. Distinct from the MES *trade*-data models (mes-execution-quality / mes-setup-quality / mes-trade-outcome-winrate) which ARE empty_dataset-blocked and self-resolve once MES trades.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: not re-verified this run (model detail moved to /ml-review per 2026-05-26 split). Per scope, /health-review only checks trainer SERVICE state — service was 'failed' on last 2026-05-27 00:28 cycle (overall_rc=1 from BL-20260526-002's MES manifest failures, which include these two). Awaits model-training session.

items.title: prop-mission-policy-baseline-v0 emits all-zero shadow scores — degenerate, prop-account-infra-blocked (NOT MES/IBKR-related)
items.description: The shadow log shows prop-mission-policy-baseline-v0 scoring 0.000 on every live signal. Root cause (ml/trainers/per_strategy_winrate.py + ml/datasets/families/account_context.py): it learns per-strategy mean was_taken acceptance rate from PROP-account trades only (accounts.yaml type:prop), but the only prop account (prop_velotrade_1) is mode:dry_run with strategies:[] and never trades — so there are no 'taken' positives, the learned rates / global_rate are 0, and live scoring of non-prop signals falls back to 0.0. NOTE: this is NOT resolved by tomorrow's IBKR/MES approval — it needs the prop account to actually trade (prop SDK wiring + a non-empty strategies list + mode:live = Tier-3/infra). Operator decision 2026-05-25: leave as-is for now.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit once a prop account is wired + trading (prop SDK), or in a model-training session.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) a prop account trades so account_context has 'taken' positives and the model learns non-degenerate per-strategy rates, or (b) the model is demoted from shadow / scoped so it no longer emits meaningless 0.000 predictions against non-prop live signals (a manifest/scoring change, Tier-3-aware).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: status snapshot confirms prop_velotrade_1 live=false (mode unchanged). Model perf moved to /ml-review scope per 2026-05-26 split. Remains operator/infra-gated.

items.title: Stale Cloudflare Pages integration fails CI on every ict-trader-dashboard commit (vestigial 'ict-trader-widget' project)
items.description: The ict-trader-dashboard repo runs a 'Cloudflare Pages' check that FAILS in ~0s on every commit/PR (seen on PR #71, commits across the session). It targets a leftover Cloudflare Pages project named 'ict-trader-widget' — a remnant of the retired React+Vercel/Cloudflare dashboard stack (PR #32 pivoted the dashboard to Streamlit; cf-worker/ was removed 2026-05-12 per CLAUDE.md). The repo no longer has any Pages build, so the Pages project has nothing to build and errors. The real CI (ruff-lint, import-smoke) is green. This is NOT fixable from the repo — there is no in-repo config that controls that Pages project; it is a Cloudflare-dashboard <-> GitHub integration. Cross-repo note: the failing check is on the DASHBOARD repo (benbaichmankass/ict-trader-dashboard), logged here because that repo has no backlog and /health-review drains this one.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; surface to the operator (external infra — cannot be auto-fixed from either repo).
items.resolution_criteria: Operator disconnects the 'ict-trader-widget' Cloudflare Pages <-> GitHub integration (or deletes that Pages project) so the dashboard repo stops running a perpetually-failing Cloudflare Pages check; confirm ict-trader-dashboard PRs show only the real checks (ruff-lint, import-smoke) green.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: external infra; nothing to verify from /health-review's read surface. Remains operator-gated.

items.source: operator flagged a live BTCUSDT trade whose TP (~143,210) sat ~2x the BTC price / above the all-time high
items.title: EXPECTED, NOT A BUG: trend_donchian (+ fade/squeeze) place a far ~50R 'unreachable sentinel' TP — don't re-flag it as a broken take-profit
items.description: A live trend_donchian BTCUSDT long showed TP=143,210.10 with SL=76,355.40 (entry ~77,666; current ~77,300). This is the DESIGNED 50R sentinel, not a malfunction. trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h have NO fixed profit target (matches their backtests, which have no TP) — the Chandelier ATR trailing stop is the sole profit exit. But the pipeline's dispatch gate requires every order to carry BOTH an SL and a TP, so the TP is parked tp_r*risk away (tp_r=50.0 in config/strategies.yaml:153/216/259) where it can't realistically fill (max single-trade excursion in 3yr backtest ~11R). Signature to recognize it: reward:risk ~= 50:1. Math check on this trade: TP = entry + 50*(entry - SL) => 77,666.27 + 50*1,310.87 = 143,209.8 ~= 143,210.10. Formula lives at src/units/strategies/trend_donchian.py:190/195 (order_package) and the trade is protected by the live ratcheting trail in monitor() at trend_donchian.py:285 (moves SL up only, never loosens). The TP-cross close at trend_donchian.py:342 exists only for completeness. A far TP carries no margin/liquidation risk; if BTC ever printed 143k the trade would simply close at +50R. The only live strategy that emits this is trend_donchian (turtle_soup uses tp1_at_r 1.0 / tp2_at_r 3.0, ict_scalp tp_at_r 1.5, vwap a VWAP-anchored TP) — fade/squeeze share the 50R sentinel and (as of 2026-05-25) also run execution: live, so their 50R TPs now appear on real bybit_2 order packages too — still the intended sentinel (see BL-20260525-008).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run that scores a trend_donchian / fade_breakout_4h / squeeze_breakout_4h order package and sees a TP far from price (~50R / ~2x BTC). Recognize it as the intended sentinel — do NOT score it as a TP defect.
items.resolution_criteria: Informational standing note; nothing to fix. Close only if the 50R-sentinel mechanism is itself removed (e.g. the pipeline stops requiring a TP for trail-only strategies, or tp_r is changed in an operator-approved Tier-3 PR).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /doc-freshness: SHORT-branch formula now clamped (PR #2141 merged 2026-05-27 09:01Z) — for shorts, tp = max(entry*0.01, entry - tp_r*risk). The clamp activates when 50R*risk > entry (the 'TP would go negative' case that hit trend_donchian at BTC ~$75k with risk ~$1500 on 2026-05-27 03:50-05:24, refused by the execute_pkg tp>0 pre-flight). Sentinel intent is unchanged — entry*0.01 is still effectively unreachable for BTC. LONG branch unaffected. Still a standing 'don't flag this as a defect' note; only the shape of the math changed for shorts.

items.source: PR #1995 (merged 2a951719, deployed 2026-05-25) — fade/squeeze promoted shadow->live + symmetric bybit routing
items.title: VERIFY fade_breakout_4h + squeeze_breakout_4h are trading CORRECTLY on bybit_2 (real money) after the 2026-05-25 shadow->live promotion
items.description: Operator-approved change (PR #1995): both bybit accounts now run the same six strategies, and fade_breakout_4h + squeeze_breakout_4h were promoted execution: shadow -> live. They now place REAL orders on bybit_2 (and paper on bybit_1 demo). Deployed + trader restarted 2026-05-25 14:12 UTC at HEAD 2a951719; clean boot confirmed (both are evaluated each tick). NOT YET PROVEN: that when a setup actually fires they dispatch a LIVE order correctly. Next health-review must verify, once each has fired >=1 live setup on bybit_2: (1) the order package dispatches LIVE (effective_dry=False) to bybit_2 — NOT silently gated to dry by the shadow gate — and paper to bybit_1; (2) a real (is_demo=false) execution reaches Bybit with correct sizing (risk_pct 0.3) + 3x leverage; (3) order_packages + trades rows are journaled correctly with the right strategy/account; (4) the Chandelier trail (monitor()) ratchets the SL on the live position; (5) vwap is STILL data-only (execution: shadow) on both accounts. ACCEPTED RISK to watch: fade/squeeze are unvalidated-live (OOS expectancy ~half of train, month-concentrated) and routing them onto bybit_2 removed the prior defense-in-depth (the per-strategy shadow gate fails open on a YAML read error, coordinator.py:1105) — so eyeball their early live PnL/behavior and flag anything off.
items.trigger_condition: Next /health-review after 2026-05-25; specifically once fade_breakout_4h and/or squeeze_breakout_4h have fired at least one live setup on bybit_2 (fade needs chop / ADX<20 failed breakout; squeeze needs a Bollinger-in-Keltner release). If neither has fired yet, keep open and re-check next run.
items.resolution_criteria: Confirmed at least one fade AND one squeeze order package dispatched live to bybit_2 with a real (non-demo) execution, correct sizing/leverage, live trail ratchet, and correct journaling — vwap still shadow. If any of these is wrong (gated dry, mis-sized, no exchange order, mis-journaled), file a finding and surface to the operator (real money).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: fade_breakout_4h DID emit signals in window (3 packages: pkg-f9cba06c0d0e48f3 long open; pkg ending 2026-05-27T05:06 long closed→trade 1763; pkg from 2026-05-26T14:54 short closed→trade 1742) — but ALL aggregated_target_qty=0.0 (same-direction reinforcement of an existing trade), so no NEW live order fired to verify against. squeeze_breakout_4h: zero packages in window (no fire). vwap (still execution: shadow) emitted 8 zero-qty reinforcement packages — correct behaviour. Net: cannot confirm fade dispatches a NEW live order yet; verification deferred to a fresh fade or squeeze fire.

items.source: Investigating the trend_donchian stuck-watchdog alert (trade 1722 / pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339); fixed the alert symptom in PR #2002
items.title: Zero-sized same-direction reinforcement signals persist as open order_packages (clutter + watchdog churn)
items.description: trend_donchian (and any breakout strategy) re-fires a same-direction signal on each new bar while the breakout persists. The intent multiplexer correctly refuses to pyramid onto an already-open position — it sizes the reinforcement to target_qty=0.0 (aggregation_reason 'same_direction_reinforcement: ... max target_qty=0.0', sized_qty_by_account={}) — but the zero-qty package is STILL persisted as status='open' with linked_trade_id set, even though no exchange order was placed. These ghost packages never close on their own; their updated_at goes stale and they accumulate in order_packages, and they are what trips the stuck-strategy watchdog (the live snapshot for trade 1722 showed pkg-ac6bb4b3b33f4339 and earlier ones already watchdog_released_alive). PR #2002 made the watchdog timeframe-aware so it no longer false-alerts on these, but the underlying clutter remains. Root fix: a reinforcement that sizes to 0 (no order to monitor) should NOT create a lingering open order_packages row — either skip persisting it, persist it with a terminal status (e.g. status='skipped'/'no_order' + close_reason), or auto-close it so it's never gated/monitored/watchdog'd. Verify the strategy_monocle gate (pipeline.py::_has_open_package_for_strategy) and the per-strategy gates still behave correctly afterward.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever order_packages shows multiple open same-direction zero-qty packages for one live position. Investigate the intent_multiplexer / order-package persistence path.
items.resolution_criteria: A same-direction reinforcement that aggregates to target_qty=0 no longer leaves a lingering status='open' order_packages row (it is skipped or persisted terminal), so ghost packages stop accumulating and the stuck-watchdog has nothing benign to flag. Order-path change (Tier-2): open a draft PR + operator review.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: STILL HAPPENING at high cadence. 21/21 packages in window (~14h) all carry aggregated_target_qty=0.0 + empty sized_qty_by_account — across 3 strategies: 8 vwap (2026-05-26T22:22–23:13), 10 trend_donchian (2026-05-27T03:50–05:24), 3 fade_breakout_4h. 18/21 have linked_trade_id=null (pure ghost), 3 link to live trades 1742/1763/1769 (reinforcement). 15 ended status=orphaned by reconciler (5min BUG-049). Two new edge cases: conflict_resolved_by_priority (e.g. vwap wins over trend_donchian, then still aggregates to 0) also produces ghost packages. Code change still pending — Tier-2.

items.source: session wrap-up — v2 BTC LightGBM regime models entered shadow at 2026-05-26 ~16:08Z + ~17:13Z (last retrain on 7015659). Promotion conversation queued for after 7-day soak.
items.title: v2 BTC LightGBM regime models — 7-day shadow-soak verification + post-soak gate-check before shadow->advisory promotion conversation
items.description: btc-regime-5m-lgbm-v2 and btc-regime-15m-lgbm-v2 were trained with PR #2067's class-rebalancing and PR #2072's 5m weight retune; PR #2068 added the imbalance-aware alternative path through `non_degenerate` so they can pass the gate despite low minority-class F1 on the imbalanced regime label. VERIFIED at session end (post-#2068 cycle on 5662f1f): both models now report `non_degenerate=pass` via the imbalance-aware path — 5m has min precision_lift=13.60 (>=2.00) + min recall=0.419 (>=0.05); 15m has min precision_lift=3.78 + min recall=0.666. Both also pass sample_sufficiency + cross_run_stability. Engineering work is fully landed; only TIME-based gates remain (shadow_soak >= 7 days; beats_baseline/live_agreement/drift_clean need live shadow predictions accumulated against closed trades). Models entered current shadow stage at 2026-05-26T16:08:23Z (15m) and 16:08:40Z (5m) per gate-check output; 7-day clock targets ~2026-06-02 16:08Z.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run on or after 2026-06-02; or any session investigating 'are the v2 BTC models ready to promote shadow->advisory?'.
items.resolution_criteria: Verified end-to-end: (1) shadow_predictions.jsonl carries entries for both model_ids tied to closed live trades (BTCUSDT only); (2) brier_lift, AUC>0.55, and a populated shadow-drift report are all present so beats_baseline/live_agreement/drift_clean clear; (3) all gates pass + the evidence packet is prepared for the operator's shadow->advisory decision. Items (1)-(2) accumulate naturally during the 7-day soak as live BTCUSDT signals fire. Operator-gated decision; Claude prepares the packet, operator authorizes.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: still snoozed (target 2026-06-02 16:08Z). Model-perf scope is /ml-review now; no action this skill.

items.source: session wrap-up — PR #2062 explicitly scoped MES manifests out; they still fail in every training cycle.
items.title: MES regime + setup-quality manifests fail every trainer cycle — need yfinance/ES=F intraday market_raw on the trainer + non-empty MES setup_labels shard at v002
items.description: PR #2062's build_btcusdt_pair helper fixed the BTCUSDT 5m/15m v002 shards but explicitly deferred the MES failures. Three manifests fail every cycle with FileNotFoundError at: datasets-out/market_features/MES/5m/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-5m.yaml), datasets-out/market_features/MES/15m/v002/data.jsonl (mes-regime-15m.yaml), and datasets-out/setup_labels/MES/all/v002/data.jsonl (mes-setup-quality.yaml). Root causes: (a) scripts/ops/build_trainer_datasets.sh only builds market_raw/features for BTCUSDT (Bybit V5 offvm adapter); MES needs the yfinance/ES=F intraday adapter — that wiring exists in the separate scripts/ops/run_mes_training.sh one-shot but isn't part of the daily cycle. (b) The cycle's setup_labels build is symbol-scope=all (not MES); a separate `build_family setup_labels --symbol-scope MES` invocation at v002 is needed (will produce an empty shard until MES trades, but an empty shard at least lets the manifest skip cleanly via empty_dataset instead of failing). Net effect: trainer cycle exits with overall_rc=1 every run even though all BTC manifests succeed.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or any session attempting to train MES regime / setup-quality models.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) build_trainer_datasets.sh extended to invoke a build_market_pair-like helper for MES using the yfinance/ES=F adapter for 5m + 15m plus a MES-scoped setup_labels build, so the three failing manifests either train (where data is present) or empty_dataset-skip (where it isn't); OR (b) the three failing manifests are explicitly scoped out of the daily cycle (e.g. via a TRAINING_MANIFESTS-exclude wrapper) so overall_rc returns 0. Cycle log on next run shows zero manifest_failed events. Pure trainer-VM tooling change; autonomous (Tier-1).
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: re-confirmed — trainer-vm-diag shows ict-trainer.service ExecMainStatus=1 on the 2026-05-27 00:28 cycle (InactiveEnterTimestamp 00:31:25). Timer still enabled+active; next fire 2026-05-28 00:02. Service-health graded WATCH in this review; manifest detail belongs to /ml-review.

items.source: /health-review 2026-05-27 — three of six diag-relay issues opened back-to-back never returned a comment
items.title: vm-diag-snapshot relay: subset of issues opened in quick succession never comment+close (stuck OPEN; 38 stale opens accumulated since 2026-05-17)
items.description: This review opened six diag issues at 2026-05-27T07:19:28..54Z. Three returned comments within 30s and auto-closed normally (#2125 status, #2128 order_packages, #2130 trainer-vm). Three never returned a comment in 16+ min and stayed OPEN (#2126 services, #2127 audit, #2129 trades). Cross-check: there are now 38 open vm-diag-request issues going back to #1327 (2026-05-17) — a chronic accumulation. Hypothesis: when issues open within seconds of each other, the concurrency-group serializer (`group: vm-diag-snapshot, cancel-in-progress: false`) starts them but a subset of runs fail/timeout SSH or curl in a way that the post-step `if: cancelled()` / `if: failure()` comment-back step doesn't fire — so the issue is left open with no comment. The 2026-05-16 commentary in vm-diag-snapshot.yml already flagged this exact failure mode (#1278/#1281/#1284). Effect: /health-review can't reliably pull a full state set in one fire; chronic open-issue clutter on the repo. Code-level fix needed in .github/workflows/vm-diag-snapshot.yml: add an always() post-step that drops a 'workflow ran but produced no output / failed at step X' comment so the issue stops looking 'in flight'.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or any session that opens >2 diag issues in quick succession and notices subsequent ones never return.
items.resolution_criteria: vm-diag-snapshot.yml carries an `if: always()` (or `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()`) closing-comment step that ALWAYS posts a result-or-error comment + closes the issue, so no diag issue can be left silently OPEN. Then sweep the 38 stale open issues — close as 'workflow run silently failed; re-open if you need the data'. Code change (Tier-1 observability); not editable from /health-review.
items.resolution: ROOT-CAUSE refined this session: vm-diag-snapshot.yml ALREADY carries `if: cancelled()` + `if: failure()` + an implicit-success handler that together cover every terminal job state. The remaining gap is when GitHub's webhook layer batches rapidly-filed `issues.opened` events and silently drops some — the workflow never starts, so none of the handlers fire. There's no in-workflow fix for that. Instead, a SEPARATE sweeper workflow `.github/workflows/diag-relay-sweep.yml` was shipped (PR #2144, merged 2026-05-27 ~09:50Z): daily cron at 06:00 UTC + on-demand workflow_dispatch; finds open `vm-diag-request` / `trainer-vm-diag-request` issues >30 min old with no `github-actions[bot]` reply, comments + closes them. REMAINING: the sweeper hasn't been verified end-to-end yet — operator should fire workflow_dispatch (dry_run=true to preview, then dry_run=false to actually close the 38 stale issues), or wait for the first cron at 2026-05-28 06:00 UTC. Once verified, mark this item fully resolved.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 session-close: sweeper workflow shipped; awaiting first run.

items.source: session wrap-up — `python -m ml compare setup-quality-baseline-v0 setup-quality-lgbm-v2` after PR #2053 + #2067 cycles.
items.title: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 underperforms v0 baseline on n=76 closed trades — too little data for LightGBM
items.description: setup-quality-lgbm-v2 trained cleanly (PR #2053's LightGBM stack works end-to-end) but loses to setup-quality-baseline-v0 on the eval split: MAE 0.0710 vs 0.0478, MSE 0.0114 vs 0.0092 (n_eval=76). Cause is dataset size, not model quality — 76 closed trades is too small for a gradient-boosted tree to beat a simple per-group-mean baseline, even with the conservative regularization PR #2053 added (min_data_in_leaf=10, num_leaves=15, lambda_l2=1.0). Promoting this model would mean shipping noise instead of signal. The model is auto-wired into the shadow factory by the 2026-05-19 default-flip rule, so it's currently logging shadow predictions on every live signal — that's fine and produces useful data, but the model itself shouldn't be considered for promotion past shadow until either it beats baseline at higher n, or the manifest is explicitly demoted/deprecated. Distinct from BL-20260526-001 (BTC regime models which DO beat their baselines on macro_f1 + the imbalance-aware path).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; revisit specifically when n_trades >= ~1000 closed trades suitable for setup_labels OR in a dedicated model-training session reviewing the v2 setup-quality manifest.
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) on a fresh training cycle with n_eval >= 1000 the v2 model beats the baseline on MAE+MSE, in which case keep at shadow and let it accumulate live shadow predictions; OR (b) the model is demoted from shadow to research_only via `python -m ml promote-stage setup-quality-lgbm-v2 --new-stage research_only` (operator-approved, semantic change to the registry); OR (c) the manifest is deprecated (removed from ml/configs/, model retired). Decision belongs to a model-training session, not this backlog item.
items.triage_note: 2026-05-27 /health-review: model-perf scope is /ml-review per 2026-05-26 split; no action this skill.

items.title: config/strategies.yaml comments on tp_r=50.0 still say 'effectively-unreachable sentinel' without mentioning the 2026-05-27 short-branch clamp
items.description: PR #2141 (merged 2026-05-27 09:01Z) clamped the short-TP formula in trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h to `max(entry*0.01, entry - tp_r*risk)` so the pre-flight tp>0 guard accepts the order at BTC prices where unclamped 50R would go negative. The comments in config/strategies.yaml around lines 149-153 (trend_donchian), 218 (fade_breakout_4h), and 265-267 (squeeze_breakout_4h) still describe the sentinel as the unclamped formula. Not a contradiction (the clamped 1%-of-entry floor IS still effectively unreachable, so the comments' INTENT is correct), but the wording could be refreshed for accuracy. config/strategies.yaml is Tier-3, so this skill cannot edit it — surface to the operator. Minor.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; or whenever a Tier-3 PR touches config/strategies.yaml for an unrelated reason and the comments can be refreshed in the same diff.
items.resolution_criteria: config/strategies.yaml comments at the tp_r entries for trend_donchian/fade_breakout_4h/squeeze_breakout_4h mention the short-branch clamp explicitly, OR the operator confirms the existing wording is good enough.

items.source: Today's IB Gateway wedge — Up 21h, upstream IB session dead.
items.title: IBC nightly auto-restart doesn't reliably recover the upstream IB session
items.description: IBC's `Auto restart time` is configured to 11:59 PM on the ib-gateway container (verified in the IBC log on issue #2165 post-recovery: `Auto restart time already set to 11:59 PM`). Yet today the container was 'Up 21 hours' with its upstream IB session entirely dead (Warning 2103 usfarm / 2105 ushmds / 2157 secdefnj all 'broken'; every API call timed out at 10s; 200/200 log lines were `remove Client <id>` with no upstream activity). Either IBC's nightly restart didn't fire, or it fired and didn't successfully re-authenticate to IB's data farms. The autonomous `vm-ib-gateway-recover` workflow is the current mitigation (Claude can dispatch it whenever the symptom recurs), but the root cause needs investigating. Candidates: 2FA timing on the nightly relogin, stale Java/Gateway state on Auto-restart that needs a hard container restart, or IB-side throttling on rapid reconnects. Probable scope: inspect IBC config (jts.ini, ibc.ini) + the docker entrypoint + gateway startup logs around the 11:59 PM window over multiple days to see if the restart fires at all.
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; investigate via gateway-logs + provision_ib_gateway.sh on the next 12-24h MES outage.
items.resolution_criteria: Either IBC's nightly auto-restart reliably re-establishes the upstream IB session (verifiable by 7 consecutive days with no `Up >20h with broken farm connections` state), or a sanctioned scheduled job replaces it (e.g. systemd timer triggering vm-ib-gateway-recover at 11:55 PM UTC, before IB's reset window).

items.source: Post-deploy journal during PR #2167 ship — first signal emitted after deploy triggered FCM push attempts that all 404'd.
items.title: FCM mobile-push notifier hits Firebase with stale / unregistered device tokens (404 NOT_FOUND)
items.description: When the trader emitted a fresh BTCUSDT VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:30Z, the M12 mobile-push notifier (src/runtime/mobile_push/notifier.py) fired FCM push attempts that came back `status=404 body={"error":{"code":404, "message":"Requested entity was not found.","status":"NOT_FOUND","details":[...FcmEr...]}}` — Google saying the device registration tokens FCM is targeting don't exist. Visible in the post-deploy journalctl on issue #2168. Means notifications aren't reaching the Android app. Either the dashboard/Android app never registered tokens after FCM init, or the tokens stored in the bot's token store are stale (device uninstall / FCM revocation / token rotation). Notifier should be marking these tokens as dead on 404 and stop retrying them; investigate whether notifier.py implements the FCM `UNREGISTERED` cleanup (remove-on-404) or silently retries. Pre-existing (the FCM key leak earlier today is unrelated — that was about the credential shape in .env, not the device tokens).
items.trigger_condition: Any /health-review run; tractable as a code-only investigation (no live-trading risk).
items.resolution_criteria: Either (a) confirm + ship a token-cleanup-on-404 path in notifier.py so dead tokens are pruned automatically, or (b) confirm the cleanup already exists and the token store has been drained, and document the operator path to re-register the Android app's FCM token. The 404 cluster should stop appearing in the journal after a fresh signal emission.

items.source: First VWAP signal after PR #2167 deploy — visible in post-deploy journalctl on issue #2168.
items.title: VWAP signal at 2026-05-27T17:57:35Z sized to target_qty=0.0
items.description: Brand-new BTCUSDT buy signal emitted at 17:57:35Z (entry 74790.9, deviation -1.36σ below VWAP, regime=weak-down/medium, policy_allow=True). Aggregated with fade_breakout_4h in same-direction reinforcement (`aggregation_reason: same_direction_reinforcement: 2 intents agree on long; max target_qty=0.0 from strategy=vwap`). But `target_qty=0.0` from the intent layer means the aggregated package size is zero — coordinator would either skip dispatch or refuse via the zero-qty gate. Could be a transient balance/risk-cap condition (daily_loss_budget exhausted, available USDT below min_balance, sizing math underflow), or a config drift. One observation, not yet a pattern. Next /performance-review window should grade the post-deploy VWAP cohort and check whether target_qty stays at zero (= effective shadow even though execution=live) or recovers.
items.trigger_condition: Next /performance-review window after at least 3 more BTCUSDT VWAP signals fire.
items.resolution_criteria: Either confirm target_qty>0 on the subsequent VWAP signals (transient single-tick artifact, close), or trace the sizing-to-zero root cause (which strategy/account/RiskManager refused, with the structured reason token from _explain_zero_sized_qty).

items.title: Operator-only follow-ups from the FCM private-key bleed
items.description:
The 2026-05-27 systemd-EnvironmentFile bleed dumped the FCM service-account PEM private key into the journalctl tail on TWO pull-and-deploy issue comments. PR #2158 shipped the scrub-env-noncompliant system-action and it ran successfully (12 lines stripped, journal warning is now gone — verified on issue #2161). What's left is operator-only because the MCP tools I have can neither delete issue comments (no delete_issue_comment) nor vacuum the journal (no journalctl vacuum system-action) nor rotate the FCM key (that's Google Cloud Console). Three steps:
  1. Delete the comments containing the leaked key (one-click `...` → Delete on each):
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2157#issuecomment-4555473441
     - https://github.com/benbaichmankass/ict-trading-bot/issues/2159#issuecomment-4555811974
  2. Rotate the FCM service-account key at Google Cloud Console (only step that actually neutralizes the exposure — the journal copies on the VM, webhook archives, and any third-party GitHub mirror still hold the old key). After rotation, re-dispatch `set-mobile-push-secrets` with the new JSON in the FCM_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON secret to update the on-disk file at ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json.
  3. Optional: `sudo journalctl --rotate && sudo journalctl --vacuum-time=1s` on the live VM to wipe historical journal copies containing the key. Reduces forensic surface; the scrub already stops future bleeds at source.
items.trigger_condition: Operator-only — no autonomous action possible. Surface every /health-review run until resolved.
items.resolution_criteria: Both leaked issue comments deleted (404 on the comment URLs); FCM service-account key rotated at Google Cloud Console; ${DATA_DIR}/fcm_service_account.json updated to the new credential and a fresh push test succeeds (kicks the notifier and confirms no 404 NOT_FOUND).
